title: Lithocarpus leptogyne
intro: Lithocarpus leptogyne is a species of tree in the beech family, Fagaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus leptogyne include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lithocarpus leptogyne is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat fever, or as a powder to treat digestive issues, with the most common herbal preparations being teas, tonics, and capsules.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus leptogyne is classified within the plant family Fagaceae, commonly known as the beech family. This classification places it among other species such as oaks and beeches. Lithocarpus leptogyne is further classified as a genus and species within the Fagaceae family. The Fagaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. The classification of Lithocarpus leptogyne is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships to other species within the Fagaceae family.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bark and leaves of Lithocarpus leptogyne are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
